description_long: This object is a sculpted guard stone in the form of a bowing man dressed in formal attire, including a suit with a sash, bow tie, and a cap. The figure stands upright on a circular plinth, with both hands clasped in front and detailed shoes visible at the base. The surface shows significant weathering, with patches of discoloration, chipping, and erosion, especially on the head and shoulders. The back of the figure is less detailed, with a visible support rod or reinforcement running vertically. The overall color is a mottled mix of gray, beige, and off-white, with some darker stains and surface deposits. The head appears to be partially broken or worn down, suggesting long-term exposure to the elements or repeated contact.
description: A weathered stone guard in the shape of a bowing man in formal attire, standing on a circular base with visible signs of age and surface wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed face, clasped hands, suit, sash, and shoes, with visible chipping on the head and chest.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the face, the arm position, and the curvature of the back, with discoloration and erosion on the shoulder.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the less detailed back, a vertical support rod, and significant surface wear and discoloration.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the side of the face, the arm position, and the rounded base, with more pronounced weathering on the upper back.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the entrance to driveways, alleyways, or along the edge of sidewalks in urban or historical districts to prevent vehicle encroachment and protect corners or building facades.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright on its base, sometimes partially embedded for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible chipping and erosion on the head, shoulders, and base, likely caused by vehicle contact, weather exposure, and possibly foot traffic. Discoloration and surface deposits are present, especially on the upper surfaces and areas most exposed to rain and pollution.

license_info.creator_display_name: Archeoplastica: il museo dei rifiuti spiaggiati
